figures.caption: Figure 2. Visual comparison of original MRI, ground truth, and predicted segmentation for four representative BraTS cases.
Figure 3. Visual comparison of original MRI, ground truth, and predicted segmentation for four ACDC cases.

Table 1. Comparison of various methods on the BraTS dataset using average Dice score. Bold values represent the best performance, while underlined values indicate the second best results. Here, the number of parameters (denoted as ’params’) is reported in millions.
Table 2. Comparison on ACDC dataset (avg. Dice score). Params in millions. Bold: best, underline: second best.

Figure 1. Architecture of LightMedSeg. The input volume is first embedded by a stride-2 GhostConv3D stem. The Global Anchor Detector and the Local Structural Prior Module (LSPM) extract spatial anchors S and a texture routing map T from the stem features. These priors guide (i) adaptive feature mixing before the encoder, (ii) anchor-conditioned, texture-routed processing at every encoder stage, (iii) multi-scale learned skip fusion, and (iv) an adaptive decoder with content-tied spatial position bias. A final ConvTranspose3D restores voxel-wise class logits at the original resolution.
